Window Replacements
Windows
Pella Lifestyle Series — Pocket Fit Inserts, 23 Units

All windows are Pella Lifestyle Series insert replacements — your existing frames stay in place and the new windows fit directly into them. This means less disruption to your interior walls and trim, a cleaner installation, and a faster process overall. All exterior colors are Iron Ore.

```
Room by Room
Living Room
Four Double Hung Windows
Insert replacements — existing frames stay in place. Double hung operation on all units. Decorative grilles on the upper sash. Interior finish in Provincial to match existing woodwork.

The yoga room windows have square interior frames but radius (arched) tops on the exterior. We've noted this and will account for it in the product order and installation approach.

Storm Doors
Doors
Storm Doors — Front & Back Entry

Your existing entry doors are staying in place. We'll be adding new storm doors at both the front and back entries to improve energy performance, weather protection, and curb appeal — without touching the doors themselves.

Quantity 2 — front entry and back entry
Size: 34" × 80" — fitted to existing openings
¾ view glass panel
Roll screen option
Color to be selected
Color selection still needed — we'll confirm this before ordering. Existing entry doors remain as-is.
Siding
Exterior
Vinyl Siding — All Four Elevations

The lower portion of each elevation will receive insulated vinyl lap siding, and the upper gable and dormer areas will get insulated vinyl cedar shake — a layered combination that gives the home a classic craftsman character. Color direction is blue to light blue, with final selection still to be confirmed. All product includes insulated backing for improved energy performance.

```
Lower sections: insulated vinyl lap siding
Upper gables and dormers: insulated vinyl cedar shake
Color: blue to light blue — final selection TBD
Soffit & fascia: R&R recommended — see note below
Estimated Coverage by Elevation
Elevation Lower (Lap) Upper (Shake) Est. Total
East~2 squares~2 squares~4 squares
South~4 squares~2 squares~6 squares
West~3 squares~3 squares~6 squares
North~4 squares~4 squares~8 squares
Total~13 squares~11 squares~24 squares
Rather than replacing all soffit and fascia, we recommend assessing the condition and repairing or replacing only what needs it — then coordinating with our trusted painting partner, BPR Painting, to paint to match the new siding color. This approach typically saves significant cost over full replacement.

Window & Door Wrapping
Exterior
Aluminum Wrapping — Existing Windows & Doors

Windows and doors we're not replacing will still receive fresh exterior wrapping — a clean aluminum or composite wrap around the frames that ties the whole exterior together and protects against water intrusion. The sunroom arch top units require custom-fabricated wraps given their unique profile.

```
Standard Windows
Basement
10 Windows — Standard Wrap
Existing frames remain. Fresh aluminum wrap installed on all 10 basement window openings.
Above Grade
6 Windows — Standard Wrap
Non-replaced above-grade windows wrapped to match new siding installation.
Sunroom / Entry Room — Arch Top Units
Arch Top Window
2-Wide with Arch Top — Custom Wrap
Requires custom-fabricated wrap to follow the radius profile.
Arch Top Window
3-Wide with Arch Top — Custom Wrap
Requires custom-fabricated wrap to follow the radius profile.
Arch Top Door System
Entry Door + 2 Sidelights with Arch Top — Custom Wrap
Full system wrap including door, both sidelights, and arch top transom area.
All arch top wraps are custom-fabricated to match the radius profile. Lead time and material cost will be reflected in your estimate.
```
Front Porch Pergola
Outdoor Structure
Pergola — Front Porch, 7' × 11'

A new pergola for the front porch area to add shade, structure, and curb appeal. Material and color are still to be finalized — pressure treated lumber offers durability and value, while cedar provides a more refined natural look. Both can be stained or painted to match the home's new exterior palette.

Dimensions: 7' × 11'
Location: front porch
Material: pressure treated or cedar — TBD
Color / finish: TBD
Material and color selections still needed before quoting. We'll follow up on this alongside the siding color decision.

What Happens Next
Next Steps
Here's Where We Go From Here

A few things are still needed from you before we can finalize the estimate. Here's the process from here.

1
We'll submit your window measurements to Pella for product pricing — this typically takes a few business days.
2
Once pricing is confirmed, we'll put together your full estimate and send it through for your review.
3
A few selections are still needed before we can finalize: storm door color, siding color (blue/light blue — specific product TBD), and pergola material and color.
4
We'll also connect with BPR Painting to coordinate the soffit and fascia scope alongside the siding work.
5
Questions in the meantime? Don't hesitate to reach out — we're happy to walk through anything.

Customer Inspiration
Vision
Customer Inspiration — AI Visualization

This image was provided by the customer — an AI-generated visualization showing the direction they'd like to take the exterior. It's not a final design plan, but gives us a clear sense of the color palette, siding style, window trim, and pergola character they're drawn to.

```
Customer-Provided AI Visualization
This is a customer-provided AI rendering — not a contractor-issued design. Actual results will vary based on product availability, field conditions, and final selections. We'll use this as a directional reference as we finalize materials and colors.
Key Takeaways from This Image
Siding color: blue-green / slate teal — lap siding on lower, cedar shake on upper
Window and door trim: dark charcoal / near-black wrapping
Pergola over front porch: dark-painted wood, open beam style
Existing brick and stonework retained as-is
Overall character: craftsman exterior with bold, modern color contrast
Design Discussion — Jonathan's Ideas
Ribbon Detail
Jonathan mentioned the idea of running a horizontal band — or "ribbon" — around the perimeter of the house at the transition point between the lower lap siding and the upper cedar shake. This trim band would visually divide the two zones and give the exterior a clean, intentional line all the way around.
This is a classic craftsman detail and works particularly well when the two siding zones are also different colors — the ribbon becomes the frame that separates them and makes each zone feel deliberate rather than accidental.
Two-Tone Color Strategy
Rather than one color throughout, Jonathan is interested in using two complementary but contrasting colors — one for the horizontal lap siding on the lower portion of the home, and a different color for the cedar shake on the upper gables and dormers.
The AI rendering above shows this concept in action — the lower lap reads as a lighter, cooler tone while the upper shake carries a slightly different hue. Combined with the ribbon trim and dark window wrapping, this layered approach gives the home strong craftsman character and real curb appeal.
Color selections for both zones — and the ribbon trim — are still TBD. We'll work through this alongside the siding product selection and coordinate with BPR Painting on any trim or soffit colors that tie it all together.
```
